renderer.h(3) Library Functions Manual renderer.h(3)

NAME

renderer.h - Terminal-to-pixel renderer and render-file output (audio + video).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct term_renderer_config_t

Typedefs


typedef struct terminal_renderer_s terminal_renderer_t
typedef struct render_file_ctx_s render_file_ctx_t

Enumerations


enum term_renderer_theme_t { TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK = 0, TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT = 1, TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO = 2 }
Terminal rendering theme.

Functions


asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const term_renderer_config_t *cfg, terminal_renderer_t **out)
asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed (terminal_renderer_t *r, const char *ansi_frame, size_t len)
const uint8_t * term_renderer_pixels (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_width_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_height_px (terminal_renderer_t *r)
int term_renderer_pitch (terminal_renderer_t *r)
void term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t *r)
void term_renderer_glyph_cache_cleanup (void)
asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char *output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t **out)
void render_file_set_audio_source (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, void *audio_media_source, void *audio_capture_rb)
asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, const char *ansi_frame, uint64_t captured_ns)
void render_file_set_snapshot_actual_duration (render_file_ctx_t *ctx, double actual_duration_sec)
asciichat_error_t render_file_destroy (render_file_ctx_t *ctx)

Detailed Description

Terminal-to-pixel renderer and render-file output (audio + video).

Supports theme-aware rendering that adapts colors based on the terminal's background theme (dark or light) for optimal readability.

Definition in file renderer.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct render_file_ctx_s render_file_ctx_t

Definition at line 55 of file renderer.h.

typedef struct terminal_renderer_s terminal_renderer_t

Definition at line 17 of file renderer.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum term_renderer_theme_t

Terminal rendering theme. Determines color palette for pixel-based ANSI text rendering. When TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO is used, theme is detected from terminal_has_dark_background() to adapt colors to user's theme.

Enumerator

TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK
Dark theme: use light colors for dark background
TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT
Light theme: use dark colors for light background
TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO
Auto-detect theme from terminal (default)

Definition at line 26 of file renderer.h.

26              {
27   TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK = 0,  
28   TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT = 1, 
29   TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO = 2,  
30 } term_renderer_theme_t;










Function
  Documentation




asciichat_error_t render_file_create (const char *
  output_path, int cols, int rows, int fps, int theme, render_file_ctx_t
  ** out)


Definition at line 28 of file renderer.c.


29                                                               {
30   log_info("[RENDER_FILE_CREATE] CALLED with output_path=%s, cols=%d, rows=%d, fps=%d, theme=%d, out=%p",
31            output_path ? output_path : "(null)", cols, rows, fps, theme, (void *)out);
32   render_file_ctx_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*ctx), render_file_ctx_t *);
33 
34   // Resolve font spec → platform-appropriate file path, family name, or bundled data.
35   char font_spec[512] = {0};
36   bool font_is_path = false;
37   const uint8_t *font_data = NULL;
38   size_t font_data_size = 0;
39   const char *raw_font = GET_OPTION(render_font);
40 
41   // Log the font option value from GET_OPTION (for WASM font debugging)
42   log_info("[WASM_FONT_GETTER] GET_OPTION(render_font) returned: '%s' (empty=%d), GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)=%d",
43            raw_font ? raw_font : "(null)", (!raw_font || raw_font[0] == '\0') ? 1 : 0, GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
44 
45   // Auto-select matrix font when --matrix flag is set, unless user explicitly overrides with --render-font
46   log_debug("raw_font=%s, matrix_rain=%d", raw_font, GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
47   if ((!raw_font || raw_font[0] == '\0') && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain)) {
48     raw_font = "matrix";
49     log_debug("render_file_create: [MATRIX] Using matrix font (enabled by --matrix flag)");
50   }
51 
52   asciichat_error_t fe =
53       platform_font_resolve(raw_font, font_spec, sizeof(font_spec), &font_is_path, &font_data, &font_data_size);
54   if (fe != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
55     log_warn("renderer: font resolution failed for '%s' — using system default",
56              raw_font ? raw_font : "(explicit system)");
57   }
58 
59   log_debug("render_file_create: Font resolved: font_spec='%s', font_is_path=%d, font_data=%p (size=%zu)", font_spec,
60             font_is_path, (void *)font_data, font_data_size);
61 
62   // Use reasonable default font size for render-file quality
63   double font_size_pt = GET_OPTION(render_font_size);
64 
65   term_renderer_config_t tr_cfg = {
66       .cols = cols,
67       .rows = rows,
68       .font_size_pt = font_size_pt,
69       .theme = (term_renderer_theme_t)theme,
70       .font_is_path = font_is_path,
71       .font_data = font_data,
72       .font_data_size = font_data_size,
73   };
74   SAFE_STRNCPY(tr_cfg.font_spec, font_spec, sizeof(tr_cfg.font_spec));
75 
76   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_create(&tr_cfg, &ctx->renderer);
77   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
78     log_error("render_file_create: term_renderer_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
79     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
80     return err;
81   }
82   int actual_width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
83   int actual_height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
84   log_info("render_file_create: GRID DIMENSIONS: %ux%u cells -> PIXEL DIMENSIONS: %dx%d px", cols, rows,
85            actual_width_px, actual_height_px);
86   log_debug("render_file_create: term_renderer created (%dx%d cells, %dx%d px)", cols, rows, actual_width_px,
87             actual_height_px);
88 
89   err = ffmpeg_encoder_create(output_path, actual_width_px, actual_height_px, fps, &ctx->encoder);
90   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
91     log_error("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder_create failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
92     term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);
93     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
94     return err;
95   }
96   log_debug("render_file_create: ffmpeg_encoder created successfully");
97 
98   // Initialize audio fields
99   ctx->audio_sample_rate = 48000; // Audio pipeline is 48kHz
100   // Allocate large buffer for snapshot mode (5 seconds at 48kHz with ~14 FPS = 3,428 samples per frame)
101   // Use 8192 to comfortably handle snapshot reads without excessive overhead
102   ctx->audio_buf_size = 8192; // Temporary buffer for reading audio (in floats)
103   ctx->audio_read_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->audio_buf_size * sizeof(float), float *);
104   ctx->audio_media_source = NULL;
105   ctx->audio_capture_rb = NULL;
106 
107   log_info("renderer: initialized encoder for %s", output_path);
108 
109   // Register render file context for debugging
110   NAMED_REGISTER_CONTEXT(ctx, "render_file_ctx", output_path, NULL);
111 
112   *out = ctx;
113   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
114 }

313     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
314   asciichat_error_t err = ffmpeg_encoder_destroy(ctx->encoder);
315   term_renderer_destroy(ctx->renderer);

asciichat_error_t render_file_write_frame
  (render_file_ctx_t * ctx, const char * ansi_frame, uint64_t
  captured_ns)


Definition at line 124 of file renderer.c.


124                                                                                                                 {
125   // Write to debug file directly to bypass logging system
126 #if !defined(NDEBUG) && !defined(_WIN32)
127   FILE *dbg = fopen("/tmp/render-debug.txt", "a");
128   if (dbg) {
129     fprintf(dbg, "[RENDER_WRITE_FRAME] Called with ctx=%p, frame_len=%zu, captured_ns=%llu\n", (void *)ctx,
130             ansi_frame ? strlen(ansi_frame) : 0, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
131     fclose(dbg);
132   }
133 #endif
134   log_info("render_file_write_frame: CALLED - ctx=%p, captured_ns=%llu", (void *)ctx, (unsigned long long)captured_ns);
135 
136   if (!ctx) {
137     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ctx is NULL");
138     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
139   }
140 
141   if (!ansi_frame) {
142     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ansi_frame is NULL");
143     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
144   }
145 
146   size_t frame_len = strlen(ansi_frame);
147   log_info("render_file_write_frame: processing frame (len=%zu)", frame_len);
148   if (frame_len > 0) {
149     log_info("  first 100 chars: %.100s", ansi_frame);
150   }
151 
152   asciichat_error_t err = term_renderer_feed(ctx->renderer, ansi_frame, frame_len);
153   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
154     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: term_renderer_feed failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
155     return err;
156   }
157 
158   const uint8_t *pixels = term_renderer_pixels(ctx->renderer);
159   int pitch = term_renderer_pitch(ctx->renderer);
160   int width_px = term_renderer_width_px(ctx->renderer);
161   int height_px = term_renderer_height_px(ctx->renderer);
162 
163   log_info("render_file_write_frame: pixels=%p pitch=%d dims=%dx%d", (void *)pixels, pitch, width_px, height_px);
164 
165   // Check pixels in multiple locations to verify content
166   if (pixels) {
167     // Check pixel at (0,0) - top left corner
168     uint8_t sample_r = pixels[0], sample_g = pixels[1], sample_b = pixels[2];
169 
170     // Check pixel at (500, 100) where we injected a test red stripe
171     size_t test_offset = 100 * pitch + 500 * 3;
172     uint8_t test_r = pixels[test_offset], test_g = pixels[test_offset + 1], test_b = pixels[test_offset + 2];
173 
174     // Check pixel at middle of screen
175     uint8_t sample_r_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch], sample_g_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 1],
176             sample_b_mid = pixels[(height_px / 2) * pitch + 2];
177 
178     log_info("  pixel[0,0]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), test_stripe[500,100]: RGB(%u,%u,%u), pixel[0,%d]: RGB(%u,%u,%u)", sample_r,
179              sample_g, sample_b, test_r, test_g, test_b, height_px / 2, sample_r_mid, sample_g_mid, sample_b_mid);
180   }
181 
182   // CRITICAL: Copy pixel buffer before encoding
183   // term_renderer_pixels() returns a pointer to the renderer's internal buffer which gets
184   // overwritten on the next frame. We must copy the data before passing to the encoder.
185   uint8_t *pixels_copy = NULL;
186   if (pixels) {
187     size_t pixel_buffer_size = (size_t)pitch * (size_t)height_px;
188     pixels_copy = SAFE_MALLOC(pixel_buffer_size, uint8_t *);
189     memcpy(pixels_copy, pixels, pixel_buffer_size);
190     log_info("render_file_write_frame: copied %zu bytes of pixel data (was at %p, now at %p)", pixel_buffer_size,
191              (void *)pixels, (void *)pixels_copy);
192 
193     // DEBUG: Inject test red stripe to verify encoder is working
194     static int frame_num = 0;
195     if (frame_num++ < 1) {
196       log_info("DEBUG: Injecting test RED vertical stripe into pixel buffer for verification");
197       // Fill a 100-pixel-wide vertical stripe with bright red at x=500
198       for (int y = 0; y < height_px; y++) {
199         for (int x = 500; x < 600 && x < width_px; x++) {
200           uint8_t *pixel = pixels_copy + y * pitch + x * 3;
201           pixel[0] = 255; // R
202           pixel[1] = 0;   // G
203           pixel[2] = 0;   // B
204         }
205       }
206     }
207   }
208 
209   err = ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(ctx->encoder, pixels_copy, pitch, captured_ns);
210   if (err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
211     log_warn("render_file_write_frame: ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame failed: %s", asciichat_error_string(err));
212   }
213 
214   // Free the pixel copy (ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame reads and converts it immediately)
215   SAFE_FREE(pixels_copy);
216 
217   // Write synchronized audio for this frame if available
218   // In snapshot mode, track actual FPS and calculate samples_per_frame dynamically
219   if (ctx && ctx->audio_read_buf && ctx->encoder) {
220     // Detect if we're in snapshot mode
221     bool snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
222     double snapshot_delay = GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay);
223 
224     int samples_per_frame;
225 
226     // Track frame timing for dynamic FPS calculation in snapshot mode
227     static uint64_t first_frame_ns = 0;
228     static int log_once = 0;
229 
230     if (first_frame_ns == 0) {
231       first_frame_ns = captured_ns;
232     }
233 
234     if (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay > 0) {
235       // Simple approach: distribute snapshot_delay * sample_rate evenly across expected ~70 frames
236       // This ensures total audio duration = snapshot_delay seconds
237       int total_target_samples = (int)(ctx->audio_sample_rate * snapshot_delay);
238       int expected_frame_count = (int)(13.6 * snapshot_delay); // 13.6 FPS is typical capture rate
239       samples_per_frame = total_target_samples / expected_frame_count;
240 
241       // Ensure minimum to avoid rounding to 0
242       if (samples_per_frame < 100) {
243         samples_per_frame = 100;
244       }
245 
246     } else {
247       // Normal/live mode: use 60 FPS baseline
248       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_sample_rate / 60;
249       if (!log_once++) {
250         log_info("[AUDIO_CALC] Normal mode: samples_per_frame=%d", samples_per_frame);
251       }
252     }
253 
254     if (samples_per_frame > ctx->audio_buf_size) {
255       samples_per_frame = ctx->audio_buf_size;
256       if (!log_once++) {
257         log_warn("[AUDIO_CALC] samples_per_frame clamped to buffer size %d", ctx->audio_buf_size);
258       }
259     }
260     if (samples_per_frame <= 0) {
261       samples_per_frame = 800; // Fallback minimum
262     }
263 
264     int samples_read = 0;
265     static int audio_eof_reached = 0;
266 
267     // Read from whichever audio source is available (prefer media source, fallback to capture)
268     if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_media_source) {
269       // Read from media source (file/URL audio) - try to get samples
270       samples_read = media_source_read_audio(ctx->audio_media_source, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
271 
272       if (samples_read <= 0) {
273         // EOF reached or error - no more audio samples available from source
274         audio_eof_reached = 1;
275         samples_read = 0;
276       }
277     } else if (!audio_eof_reached && ctx->audio_capture_rb) {
278       // Read from ring buffer (live mic capture)
279       samples_read = audio_ring_buffer_read(ctx->audio_capture_rb, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
280       if (samples_read < 0) {
281         samples_read = 0; // On error, write silence
282         audio_eof_reached = 1;
283       } else if (samples_read == 0) {
284         audio_eof_reached = 1;
285       }
286       log_debug("render_file_write_frame: reading audio from capture_rb (samples_per_frame=%d, read=%d)",
287                 samples_per_frame, samples_read);
288     }
289 
290     // Write audio: either from source or silence to fill remaining time
291     if (samples_read > 0) {
292       // We got samples from the source - write them
293       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_read);
294     } else {
295       // No samples available (EOF reached or not available) - write silence to maintain sync
296       // This ensures audio track duration matches video track duration
297       memset(ctx->audio_read_buf, 0, samples_per_frame * sizeof(float));
298       ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(ctx->encoder, ctx->audio_read_buf, samples_per_frame);
299     }
300   }
301 
302   return err;
303 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_buf_size,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_capture_rb,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_media_source,
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_read_buf, audio_ring_buffer_read(),
    render_file_ctx_s::audio_sample_rate,
    render_file_ctx_s::encoder, ffmpeg_encoder_write_audio(),
    ffmpeg_encoder_write_frame(), GET_OPTION, log_debug,
    log_info, log_once, log_warn,
    media_source_read_audio(), render_file_ctx_s::renderer,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, term_renderer_feed(),
    term_renderer_height_px(), term_renderer_pitch(),
    term_renderer_pixels(), and term_renderer_width_px().


Referenced by session_display_encode_frame().






asciichat_error_t term_renderer_create (const
  term_renderer_config_t * cfg, terminal_renderer_t ** out)


Definition at line 197 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


197                                                                                                      {
198   terminal_renderer_t *r = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(*r), terminal_renderer_t *);
199   r->cols = cfg->cols;
200   r->rows = cfg->rows;
201   r->theme = cfg->theme;
202 
203   if (FT_Init_FreeType(&r->ft_lib)) {
204     SAFE_FREE(r);
205     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType init failed");
206   }
207 
208   if (cfg->font_data && cfg->font_data_size > 0) {
209     if (FT_New_Memory_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_data, (FT_Long)cfg->font_data_size, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
210       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
211       SAFE_FREE(r);
212       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INIT, "FreeType: cannot load bundled font");
213     }
214   } else {
215     if (FT_New_Face(r->ft_lib, cfg->font_spec, 0, &r->ft_face)) {
216       FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
217       SAFE_FREE(r);
218       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NOT_FOUND, "FreeType: cannot load font '%s'", cfg->font_spec);
219     }
220   }
221 
222   // Detect if this is the matrix font (needs character mapping to Private Use Area)
223   r->is_matrix_font = (strstr(cfg->font_spec, "matrix") != NULL || strstr(cfg->font_spec, "Matrix") != NULL) ||
224                       (cfg->font_data != NULL && GET_OPTION(matrix_rain));
225   if (r->is_matrix_font) {
226     log_debug("term_renderer_create: Detected matrix font - will use Private Use Area character mapping");
227   }
228 
229   // Handle scalable vs bitmap fonts differently
230   // Bitmap fonts (like matrix) don't respond to FT_Set_Char_Size
231   // Instead, use FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes to request a specific pixel size
232   if (r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes > 0) {
233     // Bitmap font: use FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes to select appropriate size (12px is reasonable for ASCII)
234     int err = FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes(r->ft_face, 12, 12);
235     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [BITMAP] FT_Set_Pixel_Sizes(12, 12) returned %d", err);
236 
237     // If that fails, try FT_Select_Size as fallback
238     if (err != 0) {
239       err = FT_Select_Size(r->ft_face, 0); // Use first available strike
240       log_debug("term_renderer_create: [BITMAP FALLBACK] FT_Select_Size(0) returned %d", err);
241     }
242   } else {
243     // Scalable font: use FT_Set_Char_Size
244     // FT_Set_Char_Size takes 1/64pt units and DPI — supports fractional point sizes.
245     // 96 DPI is the standard screen DPI used here; the 64 factor is the 26.6 fixed-point scale.
246     int err = FT_Set_Char_Size(r->ft_face, 0, (FT_F26Dot6)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), 96, 96);
247     log_debug("term_renderer_create: [SCALABLE] FT_Set_Char_Size(size_pt=%.1f → %ld 1/64pt) returned %d",
248               cfg->font_size_pt, (long)(cfg->font_size_pt * 64.0), err);
249   }
250 
251   // For matrix fonts, try PUA glyph first (U+E900) since ASCII chars might not have bitmap data
252   // For other fonts, start with 'M'
253   int load_err;
254   if (r->is_matrix_font) {
255     log_debug("Matrix font detected: trying PUA glyph U+E900 first");
256     load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 0xE900, FT_LOAD_RENDER);
257     log_debug("FT_Load_Char(U+E900) returned %d", load_err);
258 
259     // If PUA glyph fails, try ASCII fallback
260     if (load_err != 0) {
261       log_debug("U+E900 failed, trying fallback character 'M'");
262       load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'M', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
263       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('M') returned %d", load_err);
264     }
265   } else {
266     // For non-matrix fonts, standard approach
267     load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'M', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
268     log_debug("DEBUG: FT_Load_Char('M', FT_LOAD_RENDER) returned %d", load_err);
269 
270     // For bitmap fonts (like matrix), FT_Load_Char may fail if the character doesn't exist.
271     // Try a fallback character if 'M' fails.
272     if (load_err != 0 && r->ft_face->num_fixed_sizes > 0) {
273       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('M') failed for bitmap font, trying fallback character 'A'");
274       load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 'A', FT_LOAD_RENDER);
275       log_debug("FT_Load_Char('A') returned %d", load_err);
276 
277       if (load_err != 0) {
278         // Last resort: try a PUA glyph for unknown bitmap fonts
279         log_debug("FT_Load_Char('A') also failed, trying PUA glyph U+E900");
280         load_err = FT_Load_Char(r->ft_face, 0xE900, FT_LOAD_RENDER);
281         log_debug("FT_Load_Char(U+E900) returned %d", load_err);
282       }
283     }
284   }
285 
286   // For monospace ASCII grid: use advance.x (proper character spacing) and rendered height
287   FT_Pos advance_x_26_6 = r->ft_face->glyph->advance.x;
288   log_debug("DEBUG: advance.x (26.6pt)=%ld", advance_x_26_6);
289 
290   // Calculate cell width from font metrics
291   r->cell_w = (int)(advance_x_26_6 >> 6);
292 
293   // Safeguard: for bitmap fonts, cell_w might be wrong if the font metrics are off
294   // Ensure we have a reasonable minimum width
295   if (r->cell_w <= 0) {
296     log_warn("term_renderer_create: cell_w is %d (invalid), using default 10px", r->cell_w);
297     r->cell_w = 10;
298   }
299 
300   // Only add spacing to fill entire frame if --stretch is specified
301   bool stretch_mode = GET_OPTION(stretch);
302   if (stretch_mode) {
303     r->cell_w++;
304     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (advance + 1 for stretch), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
305               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
306   } else {
307     log_debug("ADVANCE_X: value=%ld (26.6pt) → cell_w=%d (preserve aspect ratio), cols=%d → width_px=%d",
308               advance_x_26_6, r->cell_w, r->cols, r->cols * r->cell_w);
309   }
310 
311   // For both bitmap and scalable fonts, use the actual glyph bitmap height
312   // This ensures text doesn't overflow cells. Using size->metrics.height (line spacing)
313   // instead of bitmap.rows causes cells to be too large and text to overflow.
314   r->cell_h = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap.rows;
315   int raw_bitmap_top = r->ft_face->glyph->bitmap_top;
316   log_debug("DEBUG: cell_h=%d (from bitmap.rows), raw_bitmap_top=%d", r->cell_h, raw_bitmap_top);
317 
318   // Fallback: if cell_h is 0 (bitmap failed to load), use aspect ratio calculation
319   if (r->cell_h <= 0) {
320     log_warn("term_renderer_create: cell_h is 0 (FT_Load_Char failed or no bitmap), using aspect ratio fallback");
321     r->cell_h = r->cell_w * 2;
322     log_debug("FALLBACK: cell_h set to %d (2x width=%d)", r->cell_h, r->cell_w);
323   }
324 
325   // Apply aspect ratio correction unless --stretch is specified
326   // Terminal characters are typically 2:1 (height:width) to appear normal
327   // BUT: only correct if the loaded glyph height is reasonably close (±30%)
328   // This prevents huge jumps from small glyphs (e.g., 8px → 32px)
329   if (!stretch_mode && r->cell_h > 0) {
330     int corrected_h = r->cell_w * 2;
331     int min_acceptable = (corrected_h * 70) / 100;  // 70% of target
332     int max_acceptable = (corrected_h * 130) / 100; // 130% of target
333 
334     if (r->cell_h < min_acceptable || r->cell_h > max_acceptable) {
335       // Loaded height is far from expected, apply correction
336       log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Correcting cell_h from %d to %d (out of range [%d,%d], 2x width=%d)",
337                 r->cell_h, corrected_h, min_acceptable, max_acceptable, r->cell_w);
338       r->cell_h = corrected_h;
339     } else if (corrected_h != r->cell_h) {
340       // Loaded height is close to expected, fine-tune by small amount only
341       int delta = corrected_h - r->cell_h;
342       if (delta > 4 || delta < -4) {
343         // Only apply if difference is more than 4px
344         log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Fine-tuning cell_h from %d to %d (within acceptable range, 2x width=%d)",
345                   r->cell_h, corrected_h, r->cell_w);
346         r->cell_h = corrected_h;
347       } else {
348         log_debug("ASPECT_RATIO: Keeping cell_h=%d (close to target %d, delta=%d)", r->cell_h, corrected_h, delta);
349       }
350     }
351   }
352 
353   // Position baseline vertically within the cell.
354   // Baseline should be positioned at approximately 75% of cell height to allow proper
355   // glyph rendering with space both above and below. This prevents glyphs from being
356   // clipped at the cell boundaries.
357   //
358   // Baseline position: y_glyph = py + r->baseline - g->bitmap_top
359   // We want: 0 <= baseline - bitmap_top < cell_h (glyph fits within cell)
360   // Choose: baseline = (3 * cell_h) / 4 (positions at 75% height)
361   r->baseline = (3 * r->cell_h) / 4;
362   log_debug("DEBUG: baseline set to %d (75%% of cell_h=%d)", r->baseline, r->cell_h);
363 
364   r->width_px = r->cols * r->cell_w;
365   r->height_px = r->rows * r->cell_h;
366   log_debug("DEBUG: calculated dimensions: width_px=%d (cols=%d * cell_w=%d), height_px=%d (rows=%d * cell_h=%d)",
367             r->width_px, r->cols, r->cell_w, r->height_px, r->rows, r->cell_h);
368   // Pitch in bytes: each pixel is 4 bytes (RGBA), already aligned to 4-byte boundary
369   // No padding needed since 4 is already a multiple of 4
370   int base_pitch = r->width_px * 4;
371   r->pitch = base_pitch;
372   log_debug("PITCH_CALC: width_px=%d, base_pitch=%d, final_pitch=%d (RGBA, no padding needed)", r->width_px, base_pitch,
373             r->pitch);
374   log_debug("DEBUG: Allocating framebuffer: %d bytes total (%d pitch * %d height)",
375             (int)((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px), r->pitch, r->height_px);
376   r->framebuffer = SAFE_MALLOC((size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px, uint8_t *);
377 
378   r->vt = vterm_new(r->rows, r->cols);
379   r->vts = vterm_obtain_screen(r->vt);
380 
381   // Enable UTF-8 encoding so vterm correctly parses multi-byte characters
382   vterm_set_utf8(r->vt, 1);
383 
384   // Set size BEFORE reset to ensure screen is allocated with correct dimensions
385   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
386   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called with rows=%d cols=%d", r->rows, r->cols);
387 
388   vterm_screen_set_callbacks(r->vts, &g_vterm_cbs, r);
389   // Reset MUST come before any input writing to initialize state correctly
390   vterm_screen_reset(r->vts, 1);
391 
392   // Verify the size was set correctly after reset
393   int vt_rows, vt_cols;
394   vterm_get_size(r->vt, &vt_rows, &vt_cols);
395   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_get_size returned rows=%d cols=%d (expected %d,%d)", vt_rows, vt_cols, r->rows, r->cols);
396 
397   // CRITICAL: Set size AGAIN after reset, as reset may have affected it
398   vterm_set_size(r->vt, r->rows, r->cols);
399   log_debug("DEBUG: vterm_set_size called AGAIN after reset");
400 
401   *out = r;
402   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
403 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    terminal_renderer_s::baseline, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,
    terminal_renderer_s::cell_w, term_renderer_config_t::cols,
    terminal_renderer_s::cols, ERROR_INIT, ERROR_NOT_FOUND,
    term_renderer_config_t::font_data,
    term_renderer_config_t::font_data_size,
    term_renderer_config_t::font_size_pt,

    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face, terminal_renderer_s::ft_lib,
    GET_OPTION, terminal_renderer_s::height_px,
    terminal_renderer_s::is_matrix_font, log_debug,
    log_warn, terminal_renderer_s::pitch,
    term_renderer_config_t::rows, terminal_renderer_s::rows,
    SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, SET_ERRNO,
    term_renderer_config_t::theme, terminal_renderer_s::theme,
    terminal_renderer_s::vt, terminal_renderer_s::vts, and
    terminal_renderer_s::width_px.


Referenced by render_file_create().






void
  term_renderer_destroy (terminal_renderer_t * r)


Definition at line 553 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


553                                                    {
554   if (!r)
555     return;
556   vterm_free(r->vt);
557   FT_Done_Face(r->ft_face);
558   FT_Done_FreeType(r->ft_lib);
559   SAFE_FREE(r->framebuffer);
560   SAFE_FREE(r);
561 }






References terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,
    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face, terminal_renderer_s::ft_lib,
    SAFE_FREE, and terminal_renderer_s::vt.


Referenced by render_file_create(), and
    render_file_destroy().






asciichat_error_t term_renderer_feed
  (terminal_renderer_t * r, const char * ansi_frame, size_t len)


Definition at line 405 of file
    media/render/terminal.c.


405                                                                                                  {
406   // Clear framebuffer to ensure no leftover pixels from previous frames
407   uint8_t def_bg = (r->theme == TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT) ? 255 : 0;
408   memset(r->framebuffer, def_bg, (size_t)r->pitch * r->height_px);
409 
410   // Reset scrolling region and cursor position to prevent vertical shifts
411   static const char reset_sequence[] = "\033[2J\033[H\033[r";
412   vterm_input_write(r->vt, reset_sequence, sizeof(reset_sequence) - 1);
413 
414   // Count newlines to determine CRLF conversion size
415   int newline_count = 0;
416   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
417     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n')
418       newline_count++;
419   }
420 
421   // Convert LF-only line endings to CRLF (vterm expects CRLF)
422   // This fixes cursor positioning on alternate rows
423   char *fixed_frame = SAFE_MALLOC(len + newline_count, char *);
424   if (!fixed_frame) {
425     log_error("failed to allocate memory for CRLF conversion");
426     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "CRLF conversion allocation failed");
427   }
428 
429   size_t fixed_pos = 0;
430   for (size_t i = 0; i < len; i++) {
431     fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = ansi_frame[i];
432     if (ansi_frame[i] == '\n' && (i == 0 || ansi_frame[i - 1] != '\r')) {
433       // Insert carriage return before LF if not already preceded by CR
434       fixed_frame[fixed_pos - 1] = '\r';
435       fixed_frame[fixed_pos++] = '\n';
436     }
437   }
438 
439   vterm_input_write(r->vt, fixed_frame, fixed_pos);
440   SAFE_FREE(fixed_frame);
441 
442   // Calculate centering offsets to position grid in center of canvas
443   int grid_width_px = r->cols * r->cell_w;
444   int grid_height_px = r->rows * r->cell_h;
445   int offset_x = (r->width_px - grid_width_px) / 2;
446   int offset_y = (r->height_px - grid_height_px) / 2;
447 
448   static int frame_count = 0;
449   static char prev_row_sigs[52][5] = {0}; // Store signatures of previous frame's rows
450   frame_count++;
451 
452   // Log if any row signature changed to detect vertical shifts
453   if (frame_count % 10 == 0) {
454     char curr_row_sigs[52][5];
455     for (int row = 0; row < r->rows && row < 52; row++) {
456       // Get first 4 chars as signature
457       for (int col = 0; col < 4 && col < r->cols; col++) {
458         VTermScreenCell cell;
459         vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){row, col}, &cell);
460         curr_row_sigs[row][col] = (cell.chars[0] >= 32 && cell.chars[0] <= 126) ? cell.chars[0] : '.';
461       }
462       curr_row_sigs[row][4] = 0;
463     }
464     memcpy(prev_row_sigs, curr_row_sigs, sizeof(curr_row_sigs));
465   }
466 
467   for (int row = 0; row < r->rows; row++) {
468     for (int col = 0; col < r->cols; col++) {
469       VTermScreenCell cell;
470       vterm_screen_get_cell(r->vts, (VTermPos){row, col}, &cell);
471 
472       uint8_t fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb;
473       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.fg)) {
474         fr = cell.fg.rgb.red;
475         fg = cell.fg.rgb.green;
476         fb = cell.fg.rgb.blue;
477       } else {
478         fr = fg = fb = 204;
479       }
480 
481       if (VTERM_COLOR_IS_RGB(&cell.bg)) {
482         br = cell.bg.rgb.red;
483         bg = cell.bg.rgb.green;
484         bb = cell.bg.rgb.blue;
485       } else {
486         br = bg = bb = def_bg;
487       }
488 
489       int px = offset_x + col * r->cell_w, py = offset_y + row * r->cell_h;
490       for (int dy = 0; dy < r->cell_h; dy++) {
491         int y = py + dy;
492         if (y < 0 || y >= r->height_px)
493           continue;
494         uint8_t *line = r->framebuffer + y * r->pitch + px * 4;
495         for (int dx = 0; dx < r->cell_w; dx++) {
496           int x = px + dx;
497           if (x < 0 || x >= r->width_px)
498             continue;
499           line[dx * 4] = br;
500           line[dx * 4 + 1] = bg;
501           line[dx * 4 + 2] = bb;
502           line[dx * 4 + 3] = 255; // Alpha (fully opaque)
503         }
504       }
505 
506       if (cell.chars[0] && cell.chars[0] != ' ') {
507         // For matrix font, map ASCII characters to Private Use Area glyphs (U+E900-U+E91A)
508         uint32_t char_to_render = r->is_matrix_font ? matrix_char_map(cell.chars[0]) : cell.chars[0];
509 
510         // Use global glyph cache to avoid rasterizing every glyph every frame
511         FT_Bitmap *cached_bitmap = glyph_cache_get(r->ft_face, char_to_render);
512         if (cached_bitmap && cached_bitmap->width > 0 && cached_bitmap->rows > 0) {
513           // Find cache entry to get positioning offsets
514           glyph_cache_entry_t *cache_entry = NULL;
515           HASH_FIND_INT(g_glyph_cache, &char_to_render, cache_entry);
516           if (cache_entry) {
517             blit_glyph(r, cached_bitmap, px + cache_entry->bitmap_left, py + r->baseline - cache_entry->bitmap_top,
518                         fr, fg, fb, br, bg, bb);
519           }
520         }
521       }
522     }
523   }
524 
525   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
526 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    terminal_renderer_s::baseline,
    glyph_cache_entry_t::bitmap_left,
    glyph_cache_entry_t::bitmap_top, terminal_renderer_s::cell_h,
    terminal_renderer_s::cell_w, terminal_renderer_s::cols,
    ERROR_MEMORY, terminal_renderer_s::framebuffer,
    terminal_renderer_s::ft_face, terminal_renderer_s::height_px,
    terminal_renderer_s::is_matrix_font, log_error,
    terminal_renderer_s::pitch, terminal_renderer_s::rows,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, SET_ERRNO,
    TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT, terminal_renderer_s::theme,
    terminal_renderer_s::vt, terminal_renderer_s::vts, and
    terminal_renderer_s::width_px.

  term_renderer_glyph_cache_cleanup (void )
